---
title: "2023 DHW analysis for DRM survey locations"
output: html_document
date: "2024-05-01"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(ncdf4) # package for netcdf manipulation
library(raster) # package for raster manipulation
library(terra)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyterra)   # for geom_spatraster()
```

```{r}
# Download satellite map for Florida
world <- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(scale = "large", returnclass = "sf")
# Create base map of Florida
basemap <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world, lwd = 0.1, fill = "gray90") +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-83.2, -79.8), ylim = c(24.3, 27.3), expand = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(high = "firebrick1", mid = "yellow", low = "forestgreen", 
                       midpoint = 0.5, limits = c(0, 1), na.value = NA,
                       labels = scales::label_percent(), name = "Corals\nbleached") +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "lightsteelblue1"),
        panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill=NA),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = c(0.2, 0.5),
        legend.background = element_blank())


# Create reef hull/polygon surrounding all surveyed sites
all_survey_pts0 <- read_csv("data/drm/FRRP_EXPORT.csv") %>%
  distinct(Latitude, Longitude)

all_survey_pts <- sf::st_as_sf(x = all_survey_pts0, coords = c('Longitude', 'Latitude'))
reef_hull <- sf::st_concave_hull(sf::st_union(all_survey_pts), ratio = 0.09)
reef_hull <- sf::st_buffer(reef_hull, dist = 0.015)
reef_hull <- reef_hull %>% sf::st_set_crs(4326)
saveRDS(reef_hull, "data/spatial/reef_hull.rds")
all_survey_pts <- all_survey_pts %>% sf::st_set_crs(4326)



basemap +
  geom_sf(data = reef_hull, fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_sf(data = all_survey_pts, size = 1, alpha = 0.05) +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-83.2, -79.8), ylim = c(24.3, 27.3), expand = FALSE)




# Other helper functions
# 0) One-time setup: make sure SST and MMM are on the same grid
# This replaces all your index gymnastics. Terra will reproject/resample MMM to match SST if needed.

# parse date from coraltemp_v3.1_YYYYMMDD.nc
file_date <- function(f) as.Date(str_match(basename(f), "(\\d{8})")[,2], "%Y%m%d")

# your extent (same box you used)
fl_ext <- terra::ext(-83.525, -78.975, 23.975, 28.025)
```

DHW data downloaded 5/1/24 using:
wget -r -l1 -A "ct*" https://www.star.nesdis.noaa.gov/pub/sod/mecb/crw/data/5km/v3.1_op/nc/v1.0/daily/dhw/2023/

netcdf tutorial here: https://rpubs.com/boyerag/297592

# 2023 CRW DHW data

```{r}
# Import all NetCDF files for each date from June 1 through December 31, 2023
nc.files <- list.files("data/dhw/2023", pattern = "*.nc$", full.names = TRUE)


# Get 2023 DRM survey site coordinates
# Import data
drmsites <- read_csv("data/drm/export.csv") %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), as_factor),
         Date = as_date(as.character(Date), format = "%m/%d/%Y")) %>%
  filter(year(Date) == "2023") %>%
  distinct(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude)
# drmsites2 <- read_csv("data/drm/FRRP_EXPORT.csv") %>%
#   mutate(across(where(is.character), as_factor),
#          Date = as_date(as.character(Date), format = "%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S")) %>%
#   filter(year(Date) == "2024") %>%
#   distinct(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude)

# Open all DHW NCDF4 files
nc.data <- tibble(file = nc.files) %>%
  mutate(date = map_chr(file, ~str_extract(., "2023\\d+"), format = "%Y%m%d"),
         data = map(file, nc_open),
      dhw.all = map(data, ~ncvar_get(., "degree_heating_week", start = c(1930, 1240, 1), count = c(91, 81, 1))),
            r = map(dhw.all, ~raster(t(.), xmn = -83.525, xmx = -78.975, ymn = 23.975, ymx = 28.025,
                                     crs = CRS("+proj=longlat +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +no_defs+ towgs84=0,0,0"))),
       coords = list(drmsites),
          dhw = map2(r, coords, ~raster::extract(.x, SpatialPoints(.y[,3:4]), method = "simple")))


###### GET NEAREST PIXEL INSTEAD OF NEAREST SITE
buf_m <- 6000  #

extract_dhw <- function(r, df_xy, buf = buf_m) {
  sp <- SpatialPoints(df_xy[, c("Longitude","Latitude")], proj4string = crs(r))

  v_simple <- raster::extract(r, sp, method = "simple")

  # buffered mean ignoring NA; will still be NA if *all* nearby cells are NA
  v_buf <- raster::extract(r, sp, buffer = buf, fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)

  # use simple unless it's NA, then buffered
  ifelse(is.na(v_simple), v_buf, v_simple)
}

nc.data <- nc.data %>%
  mutate(
    dhw = map(r, ~extract_dhw(.x, drmsites, buf = buf_m))
  )

out2 <- nc.data %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(date, "%Y%m%d"),
    site = list(drmsites %>% dplyr::select(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude)),
    # sanity check: dhw vector length should equal nrow(drmsites)
    n_dhw = map_int(dhw, length),
    n_site = map_int(site, nrow)
  ) %>%
  # optional: stop early if something doesn't match
  filter(n_dhw == n_site) %>%
  dplyr::select(date, site, dhw) %>%
  unnest(cols = c(site, dhw))
```

```{r}
# Plot DHWs for all sites
ggplot(out2, aes(x = date, y = dhw, color = Subregion, group = Site)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "month", date_labels = "%b")
```

```{r}
# Write DHW data to file
write_csv(out2, file = "data/processed/2023_dhw_processed.csv")

nc.data
```


```{r}
# Map DHW for all reef pixels not just surveyed sites
# Read DHW from all files as a multi-layer SpatRaster
# (each file contributes one layer; varname must match exactly)
dhw_stack <- rast(nc.files, subds = "degree_heating_week")


# Optional: keep only June 1–Dec 31 if your folder includes other dates
# (assumes filename contains YYYYMMDD like ct5km_dhw_v3.1_20230901.nc)
dates <- as.Date(str_extract(basename(nc.files), "2023\\d{4}"), format = "%Y%m%d")
keep  <- dates >= as.Date("2023-06-01") & dates <= as.Date("2023-12-31")
dhw_stack <- dhw_stack[[which(keep)]]

# Crop to your Florida Reef Tract box (match your plotting extent-ish)
dhw_stack <- crop(dhw_stack, ext(-83.525, -78.975, 23.975, 28.025))
dhw_stack_crw_2023 <- dhw_stack

# Peak DHW (max over time) for each pixel
peak_dhw <- app(dhw_stack, fun = max, na.rm = TRUE)
names(peak_dhw) <- "peak_dhw"


# Mask to reef pixels hull
peak_dhw_reef <- mask(peak_dhw, vect(reef_hull))

# Save reef pixel hull for later use
# choose which one you want to treat as "the DHW grid"
dhw_grid <- peak_dhw_reef  # or peak_dhw_reef
writeRaster(
  dhw_grid,
  filename = "data/spatial/reef_pixel_grid.tif",
  overwrite = TRUE
)

# Plot as heatmap
dhwmap_pixels23 <- basemap +
  geom_spatraster(data = peak_dhw_reef) +   # or peak_dhw if you don't mask
  geom_sf(data = world, linewidth = 0.1, fill = "gray90") +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-83.2, -79.8), ylim = c(24.3, 27.3), expand = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "Spectral",
    limits = c(0, 23),
    na.value = NA,
    name = "Peak\nDHW      "
  )

dhwmap_pixels23
```


# RECALC CRW-MATCHED DHW VALUES FROM SST
```{r}
library(ncdf4)

one_sst_file <- "data/coraltemp/coraltemp_v3.1_20240923.nc"
clim_file    <- "data/coraltemp/ct5km_climatology_v3.1.nc"

# SST subgrid indices (your established window)
x_start_sst <- 1931
y_start_sst <- 2280
x_n_sst     <- 90
y_n_sst     <- 82

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Read SST lon/lat + SST subgrid (RAW)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
nc_s <- nc_open(one_sst_file)

lon_s_full <- ncvar_get(nc_s, "lon")
lat_s_full <- ncvar_get(nc_s, "lat")

lon_s <- lon_s_full[x_start_sst:(x_start_sst + x_n_sst - 1)]
lat_s <- lat_s_full[y_start_sst:(y_start_sst + y_n_sst - 1)]

sst <- ncvar_get(nc_s, "analysed_sst",
                 start = c(x_start_sst, y_start_sst, 1),
                 count = c(x_n_sst,     y_n_sst,     1))
if (length(dim(sst)) == 3) sst <- sst[, , 1]
fill_sst <- ncatt_get(nc_s, "analysed_sst", "_FillValue")$value
nc_close(nc_s)

sst[sst == fill_sst] <- NA_real_

stopifnot(length(lon_s) == nrow(sst))
stopifnot(length(lat_s) == ncol(sst))

cat("SST dim:", paste(dim(sst), collapse=" x "), "\n")
cat("SST lon range:", range(lon_s), "\n")
cat("SST lat range:", range(lat_s), "\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Read MMM lon/lat + map SST lon/lat to MMM indices
#    Robust approach: read bounding rectangle, then subset to exact ix/iy
# ------------------------------------------------------------
nc_c <- nc_open(clim_file)
lon_c <- ncvar_get(nc_c, "lon")
lat_c <- ncvar_get(nc_c, "lat")

dx <- median(diff(lon_c))
dy <- median(diff(lat_c))
cat("MMM grid dx:", dx, " dy:", dy, "\n")

# Map lon/lat -> MMM indices using origin+step (works even if dy is negative)
ix <- round((lon_s - lon_c[1]) / dx) + 1
iy <- round((lat_s - lat_c[1]) / dy) + 1

# bounds check
stopifnot(all(ix >= 1 & ix <= length(lon_c)))
stopifnot(all(iy >= 1 & iy <= length(lat_c)))

# alignment check (tolerance)
tol <- 1e-4
cat("Max |lon diff|:", max(abs(lon_c[ix] - lon_s), na.rm=TRUE), "\n")
cat("Max |lat diff|:", max(abs(lat_c[iy] - lat_s), na.rm=TRUE), "\n")
stopifnot(max(abs(lon_c[ix] - lon_s), na.rm=TRUE) <= tol)
stopifnot(max(abs(lat_c[iy] - lat_s), na.rm=TRUE) <= tol)

# Order indices so extracted MMM ends up in increasing lon/lat order
ix2 <- ix[order(lon_c[ix])]
iy2 <- iy[order(lat_c[iy])]

# IMPORTANT: do NOT require contiguity. Instead:
# 1) read bounding rectangle that contains all needed indices
x0 <- min(ix2); xN <- max(ix2) - x0 + 1
y0 <- min(iy2); yN <- max(iy2) - y0 + 1

mmm_block <- ncvar_get(nc_c, "sst_clim_mmm",
                       start = c(x0, y0, 1),
                       count = c(xN, yN, 1))
if (length(dim(mmm_block)) == 3) mmm_block <- mmm_block[, , 1]

fill_mmm <- ncatt_get(nc_c, "sst_clim_mmm", "_FillValue")$value
nc_close(nc_c)
mmm_block[mmm_block == fill_mmm] <- NA_real_

# 2) subset bounding rectangle down to the exact target index vectors
# convert global indices -> local indices within the block
ix_local <- ix2 - x0 + 1
iy_local <- iy2 - y0 + 1

mmm <- mmm_block[ix_local, iy_local]

cat("MMM dim:", paste(dim(mmm), collapse=" x "), "\n")
stopifnot(all(dim(mmm) == dim(sst)))

cat("✅ MMM and SST aligned (dims + lon/lat order).\n")



# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) HotSpot and daily DHW contribution (for this one SST day)
#    (CRW-style rules)
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# Basic checks
stopifnot(all(dim(sst) == dim(mmm)))
stopifnot(identical(dim(sst), dim(mmm)))

# HotSpot in degC (can be negative)
hotspot_raw <- sst - mmm

# CRW convention: HotSpot cannot be < 0
hotspot <- pmax(hotspot_raw, 0)

# DHW only accumulates when HotSpot >= 1°C
# Convert daily HotSpot (°C) to "degree heating weeks per day" by / 7
dhd <- ifelse(hotspot >= 1, hotspot / 7, 0)

cat("HotSpot raw summary:\n")
print(summary(as.vector(hotspot_raw)))

cat("\nHotSpot (clipped at 0) summary:\n")
print(summary(as.vector(hotspot)))

cat("\nDaily DHW contribution (HotSpot>=1, divided by 7) summary:\n")
print(summary(as.vector(dhd)))

# A couple of useful diagnostics
cat("\nProportion of valid cells with HotSpot>=1:\n")
prop_ge1 <- mean(hotspot >= 1, na.rm = TRUE)
print(prop_ge1)

cat("\nRange checks (sanity):\n")
cat("SST range:", range(sst, na.rm=TRUE), "\n")
cat("MMM range:", range(mmm, na.rm=TRUE), "\n")
cat("HotSpot range:", range(hotspot, na.rm=TRUE), "\n")
```

```{r}
library(ncdf4)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(slider)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# CONFIG: your aligned grid objects + your SST window indices
# (assumes lon_s, lat_s, mmm already exist from prior steps)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
sst_dir <- "data/coraltemp"

x_start_sst <- 1931
y_start_sst <- 2280
x_n_sst     <- 90
y_n_sst     <- 82

stopifnot(all(dim(mmm) == c(x_n_sst, y_n_sst)))

# Helper: parse date from filename coraltemp_v3.1_YYYYMMDD.nc
file_date <- function(f) as.Date(str_match(basename(f), "(\\d{8})")[,2], "%Y%m%d")

# Helper: read SST subgrid (RAW) for one file
read_sst_window <- function(f) {
  nc <- nc_open(f)
  on.exit(nc_close(nc), add = TRUE)

  sst <- ncvar_get(nc, "analysed_sst",
                   start = c(x_start_sst, y_start_sst, 1),
                   count = c(x_n_sst,     y_n_sst,     1))
  if (length(dim(sst)) == 3) sst <- sst[, , 1]

  fill <- ncatt_get(nc, "analysed_sst", "_FillValue")$value
  sst[sst == fill] <- NA_real_
  sst
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Choose date range (IMPORTANT: include >=84 days before
#    the first day you care about, otherwise early DHW is wrong)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
date_min <- as.Date("2024-03-01")  # gives cushion before June
date_max <- as.Date("2024-12-31")

sst_files <- list.files(sst_dir, pattern = "^coraltemp_v3\\.1_\\d{8}\\.nc$", full.names = TRUE)
sst_tbl <- tibble(file = sst_files, date = map(file, file_date) |> list_c()) %>%
  filter(date >= date_min, date <= date_max) %>%
  arrange(date)

stopifnot(nrow(sst_tbl) > 0)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Build per-day DHW contribution (dhd) for each pixel
#    dhd = (HotSpot / 7) if HotSpot >= 1, else 0
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# We'll keep indices x=1..90, y=1..82 as pixel IDs (fast + stable).
dhd_long <- sst_tbl %>%
  mutate(
    sst_mat = map(file, read_sst_window),
    dhd_mat = map(sst_mat, ~{
      stopifnot(all(dim(.x) == dim(mmm)))
      hotspot <- pmax(.x - mmm, 0)
      ifelse(hotspot >= 1, hotspot / 7, 0)
    })
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(date, dhd_mat) %>%
  mutate(dhd_long = map(dhd_mat, ~{
    as.data.frame(as.table(.x)) %>%
      rename(x = Var1, y = Var2, dhd = Freq) %>%
      mutate(
        x = as.integer(x),
        y = as.integer(y)
      )
  })) %>%
  dplyr::select(date, dhd_long) %>%
  unnest(dhd_long)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Compute DHW per pixel as rolling 84-day sum of dhd
#    (CRW: 12 weeks = 84 days; include current day)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
dhw_long <- dhd_long %>%
  group_by(x, y) %>%
  arrange(date, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    dhw = slide_index_sum(dhd, date, before = 83L, complete = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Quick sanity check for your example date
dhw_long %>%
  filter(date == as.Date("2024-09-23")) %>%
  summarize(
    dhw_min = min(dhw, na.rm=TRUE),
    dhw_med = median(dhw, na.rm=TRUE),
    dhw_max = max(dhw, na.rm=TRUE),
    n_na    = sum(is.na(dhw))
  ) %>%
  print()
```

```{r}
# Compare to CRW DHW netcdf product
library(ncdf4)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Inputs you already have:
# - lon_s, lat_s : SST window lon/lat vectors (lengths = 90, 82)
# - x_n_sst, y_n_sst : 90, 82
# - dhw_long : your computed DHW long table
# ------------------------------------------------------------

target_date <- as.Date("2024-09-23")

# ---- point this to the CRW DHW file for the SAME date ----
# Example naming; adjust pattern/path to your real DHW directory + filenames
dhw_dir  <- "data/dhw/2024"   # <--- change if needed
dhw_file <- list.files(dhw_dir, pattern = "20240923.*\\.nc$", full.names = TRUE)[1]
stopifnot(!is.na(dhw_file), file.exists(dhw_file))

# Helper to parse date (optional, just for checks)
file_date <- function(f) as.Date(str_match(basename(f), "(\\d{8})")[,2], "%Y%m%d")

stopifnot(file_date(dhw_file) == target_date)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Read DHW lon/lat vectors + map SST lon/lat to DHW indices
#    Robust mapping: index = round((coord - origin)/step)+1
# ------------------------------------------------------------
nc_d <- nc_open(dhw_file)
on.exit(nc_close(nc_d), add = TRUE)

lon_d <- ncvar_get(nc_d, "lon")
lat_d <- ncvar_get(nc_d, "lat")

dx_d <- median(diff(lon_d))
dy_d <- median(diff(lat_d))

# map SST lon/lat vectors onto DHW indices
ix <- round((lon_s - lon_d[1]) / dx_d) + 1
iy <- round((lat_s - lat_d[1]) / dy_d) + 1

# bounds + alignment tolerance checks
stopifnot(all(ix >= 1 & ix <= length(lon_d)))
stopifnot(all(iy >= 1 & iy <= length(lat_d)))

tol <- 1e-4
cat("Max |lon diff|:", max(abs(lon_d[ix] - lon_s), na.rm=TRUE), "\n")
cat("Max |lat diff|:", max(abs(lat_d[iy] - lat_s), na.rm=TRUE), "\n")
stopifnot(max(abs(lon_d[ix] - lon_s), na.rm=TRUE) <= tol)
stopifnot(max(abs(lat_d[iy] - lat_s), na.rm=TRUE) <= tol)

# order to increasing lon/lat (so x=1..90, y=1..82 consistent)
ix2 <- ix[order(lon_d[ix])]
iy2 <- iy[order(lat_d[iy])]

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Read bounding rectangle from CRW DHW, then subset to exact ix/iy
# ------------------------------------------------------------
x0 <- min(ix2); xN <- max(ix2) - x0 + 1
y0 <- min(iy2); yN <- max(iy2) - y0 + 1

dhw_block <- ncvar_get(nc_d, "degree_heating_week",
                       start = c(x0, y0, 1),
                       count = c(xN, yN, 1))
if (length(dim(dhw_block)) == 3) dhw_block <- dhw_block[, , 1]

fill_dhw <- ncatt_get(nc_d, "degree_heating_week", "_FillValue")$value
dhw_block[dhw_block == fill_dhw] <- NA_real_

ix_local <- ix2 - x0 + 1
iy_local <- iy2 - y0 + 1

dhw_crw_mat <- dhw_block[ix_local, iy_local]
stopifnot(all(dim(dhw_crw_mat) == c(x_n_sst, y_n_sst)))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Convert to long form and compare to your computed DHW
# ------------------------------------------------------------
crw_dhw_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(dhw_crw_mat)) %>%
  rename(x = Var1, y = Var2, dhw_crw = Freq) %>%
  mutate(
    x = as.integer(x),
    y = as.integer(y),
    date = target_date
  )

cat("\nCRW DHW summary:\n")
print(crw_dhw_long %>%
  summarize(
    dhw_min = min(dhw_crw, na.rm=TRUE),
    dhw_med = median(dhw_crw, na.rm=TRUE),
    dhw_max = max(dhw_crw, na.rm=TRUE),
    n_na    = sum(is.na(dhw_crw))
  ))

comp <- dhw_long %>%
  filter(date == target_date) %>%
  dplyr::select(x, y, dhw) %>%
  left_join(crw_dhw_long, by = c("x", "y")) %>%
  mutate(diff = dhw - dhw_crw)

cat("\nDiff (yours - CRW) summary:\n")
print(comp %>%
  summarize(
    diff_min = min(diff, na.rm=TRUE),
    diff_med = median(diff, na.rm=TRUE),
    diff_max = max(diff, na.rm=TRUE),
    mae      = mean(abs(diff), na.rm=TRUE),
    rmse     = sqrt(mean(diff^2, na.rm=TRUE))
  ))

# optional: how many pixels differ by >0.1 DHW?
cat("\nPixels with |diff| > 0.1 DHW:\n")
print(mean(abs(comp$diff) > 0.1, na.rm=TRUE))
```



# do it for 2014/2015/2023/2024?
```{r}
library(ncdf4)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(slider)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: parse date from filename coraltemp_v3.1_YYYYMMDD.nc
# ------------------------------------------------------------
file_date <- function(f) as.Date(str_match(basename(f), "(\\d{8})")[,2], "%Y%m%d")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: read SST lon/lat window + SST matrix (RAW)
# Returns list(sst, lon_s, lat_s, fill)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
read_sst_window <- function(sst_file, x_start_sst, y_start_sst, x_n_sst, y_n_sst) {
  nc_s <- nc_open(sst_file)
  on.exit(nc_close(nc_s), add = TRUE)

  lon_s_full <- ncvar_get(nc_s, "lon")
  lat_s_full <- ncvar_get(nc_s, "lat")

  lon_s <- lon_s_full[x_start_sst:(x_start_sst + x_n_sst - 1)]
  lat_s <- lat_s_full[y_start_sst:(y_start_sst + y_n_sst - 1)]

  sst <- ncvar_get(nc_s, "analysed_sst",
                   start = c(x_start_sst, y_start_sst, 1),
                   count = c(x_n_sst,     y_n_sst,     1))
  if (length(dim(sst)) == 3) sst <- sst[, , 1]

  fill_sst <- ncatt_get(nc_s, "analysed_sst", "_FillValue")$value
  sst[sst == fill_sst] <- NA_real_

  # match your working assumptions: rows=lon, cols=lat
  stopifnot(length(lon_s) == nrow(sst))
  stopifnot(length(lat_s) == ncol(sst))

  list(sst = sst, lon_s = lon_s, lat_s = lat_s, fill_sst = fill_sst)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# MMM aligned to SST using YOUR WORKING METHOD:
#   ix = round((lon_s - lon_c[1]) / dx) + 1
#   iy = round((lat_s - lat_c[1]) / dy) + 1
# then bounding rectangle + local subsetting
#
# Returns list(mmm, lon_s, lat_s, sst_reordered, ord_x, ord_y)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
read_mmm_aligned_to_sst <- function(clim_file,
                                   lon_s, lat_s,
                                   sst_mat = NULL,
                                   tol = 1e-4) {

  nc_c <- nc_open(clim_file)
  on.exit(nc_close(nc_c), add = TRUE)

  lon_c <- ncvar_get(nc_c, "lon")
  lat_c <- ncvar_get(nc_c, "lat")

  dx <- median(diff(lon_c))
  dy <- median(diff(lat_c))

  # Map lon/lat -> MMM indices using origin+step (dy can be negative)
  ix <- round((lon_s - lon_c[1]) / dx) + 1
  iy <- round((lat_s - lat_c[1]) / dy) + 1

  # bounds
  stopifnot(all(ix >= 1 & ix <= length(lon_c)))
  stopifnot(all(iy >= 1 & iy <= length(lat_c)))

  # alignment check (same as your working code)
  stopifnot(max(abs(lon_c[ix] - lon_s), na.rm = TRUE) <= tol)
  stopifnot(max(abs(lat_c[iy] - lat_s), na.rm = TRUE) <= tol)

  # Order indices so extracted MMM ends up in increasing lon/lat order
  ord_x <- order(lon_c[ix])
  ord_y <- order(lat_c[iy])

  ix2 <- ix[ord_x]
  iy2 <- iy[ord_y]

  # Read bounding rectangle, then subset exact indices
  x0 <- min(ix2); xN <- max(ix2) - x0 + 1
  y0 <- min(iy2); yN <- max(iy2) - y0 + 1

  mmm_block <- ncvar_get(nc_c, "sst_clim_mmm",
                         start = c(x0, y0, 1),
                         count = c(xN, yN, 1))
  if (length(dim(mmm_block)) == 3) mmm_block <- mmm_block[, , 1]

  fill_mmm <- ncatt_get(nc_c, "sst_clim_mmm", "_FillValue")$value
  mmm_block[mmm_block == fill_mmm] <- NA_real_

  ix_local <- ix2 - x0 + 1
  iy_local <- iy2 - y0 + 1

  mmm <- mmm_block[ix_local, iy_local]

  # Reorder lon/lat (and SST if provided) to match the ix2/iy2 ordering.
  # This is the simplest way to guarantee sst and mmm are on identical orientation.
  lon_s2 <- lon_s[ord_x]
  lat_s2 <- lat_s[ord_y]

  sst2 <- sst_mat
  if (!is.null(sst_mat)) {
    stopifnot(nrow(sst_mat) == length(lon_s))
    stopifnot(ncol(sst_mat) == length(lat_s))
    sst2 <- sst_mat[ord_x, ord_y]
  }

  list(mmm = mmm, lon_s = lon_s2, lat_s = lat_s2, sst = sst2, ord_x = ord_x, ord_y = ord_y,
       dx = dx, dy = dy)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Compute DHW for multiple years, all dates in a seasonal window.
# add_by_year: named numeric vector or list; MMM_shift = MMM + add_by_year[year]
#
# IMPORTANT: includes 83-day cushion before start_mmdd so DHW at start is correct.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
add_by_year <- c(
  "2014" = 0,
  "2015" = 0,
  "2023" = 0,
  "2024" = 0
)
years_keep <- c(2014, 2015, 2023, 2024)
start_mmdd = "06-01"
end_mmdd   = "12-31"

compute_dhw_years <- function(years,
                              add_by_year = NULL,
                              sst_dir,
                              clim_file,
                              x_start_sst, y_start_sst, x_n_sst, y_n_sst,
                              start_mmdd = "06-01",
                              end_mmdd   = "12-31") {

  # Coerce indexing/count args to integer (prevents identical()/type weirdness)
  x_start_sst <- as.integer(x_start_sst)
  y_start_sst <- as.integer(y_start_sst)
  x_n_sst     <- as.integer(x_n_sst)
  y_n_sst     <- as.integer(y_n_sst)

  # normalize add_by_year
  if (is.null(add_by_year)) add_by_year <- setNames(rep(0, length(years)), as.character(years))
  if (is.list(add_by_year)) add_by_year <- unlist(add_by_year)
  if (is.null(names(add_by_year))) stop("add_by_year must be named by year, e.g., c('2014'=0, '2015'=0.2, ...)")

  # list all available SST files once
  all_files <- list.files(sst_dir, pattern = "^coraltemp_v3\\.1_\\d{8}\\.nc$", full.names = TRUE)
  all_tbl <- tibble(file = all_files, date = map(file, file_date) |> list_c()) %>%
    arrange(date)

  # compute per year
  out <- map_dfr(years, function(yy) {

    yy_int <- as.integer(yy)
    yy_chr <- as.character(yy)

    add_val <- if (yy_chr %in% names(add_by_year)) add_by_year[[yy_chr]] else 0

    start_date <- as.Date(sprintf("%d-%s", yy_int, start_mmdd))
    end_date   <- as.Date(sprintf("%d-%s", yy_int, end_mmdd))

    # cushion for 12-week rolling window (84 days incl current day => before = 83)
    date_min <- start_date - 83L
    date_max <- end_date

    sst_tbl <- all_tbl %>%
      filter(date >= date_min, date <= date_max)

    if (nrow(sst_tbl) == 0) {
      stop("No SST files found for year ", yy_chr, " in requested date window.")
    }

    # Use the first SST file to define canonical lon/lat and build MMM once per year
    first_file <- sst_tbl$file[1]
    s0 <- read_sst_window(first_file, x_start_sst, y_start_sst, x_n_sst, y_n_sst)

    # MMM aligned, and reorder SST orientation to match MMM ordering
    a0 <- read_mmm_aligned_to_sst(
      clim_file = clim_file,
      lon_s     = s0$lon_s,
      lat_s     = s0$lat_s,
      sst_mat   = s0$sst
    )

    mmm_base  <- a0$mmm
    mmm_shift <- mmm_base + add_val  # year-specific MMM shift happens here

    # sanity (non-type-strict)
    stopifnot(all(dim(mmm_shift) == c(x_n_sst, y_n_sst)))

    # Function to read + reorder SST for ANY file using the same ord_x/ord_y
    read_sst_reordered <- function(f) {
      s <- read_sst_window(f, x_start_sst, y_start_sst, x_n_sst, y_n_sst)$sst
      s[a0$ord_x, a0$ord_y]
    }

    # Compute daily dhd (HotSpot>=1 => HotSpot/7 else 0), then 84-day rolling sum
    dhd_long <- sst_tbl %>%
      mutate(
        sst_mat = map(file, read_sst_reordered),
        dhd_mat = map(sst_mat, ~{
          stopifnot(all(dim(.x) == dim(mmm_shift)))
          hotspot <- pmax(.x - mmm_shift, 0)
          ifelse(hotspot >= 1, hotspot / 7, 0)
        }),
        # IMPORTANT: do NOT include `date` inside this nested tibble (prevents unnest name collisions)
        dhd_long = map(dhd_mat, ~{
          as.data.frame(as.table(.x)) %>%
            rename(x = Var1, y = Var2, dhd = Freq) %>%
            mutate(
              x = as.integer(x),
              y = as.integer(y)
            )
        })
      ) %>%
      dplyr::select(date, dhd_long) %>%
      unnest(dhd_long)

    dhw_long <- dhd_long %>%
      group_by(x, y) %>%
      arrange(date, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
      mutate(
        dhw = slide_index_sum(dhd, date, before = 83L, complete = TRUE)
      ) %>%
      ungroup() %>%
      mutate(year = yy_int, mmm_add = add_val)

    # keep only the “analysis window” (drop cushion days)
    dhw_long %>%
      filter(date >= start_date, date <= end_date)
  })

  out
}

# ============================================================
# EXAMPLE RUN (you already have these values):
# years_keep <- c(2014, 2015, 2023, 2024)
# add_by_year <- c("2014"=0, "2015"=0, "2023"=0, "2024"=0)  # or any shifts you want
# ============================================================
dhw_long_all <- compute_dhw_years(
  years = years_keep,
  add_by_year = add_by_year,
  sst_dir = "data/coraltemp",
  clim_file = "data/coraltemp/ct5km_climatology_v3.1.nc",
  x_start_sst = x_start_sst, y_start_sst = y_start_sst,
  x_n_sst = x_n_sst, y_n_sst = y_n_sst,
  start_mmdd = start_mmdd,
  end_mmdd = end_mmdd
)

# Build canonical lon/lat for your window ONCE
one_sst_file <- "data/coraltemp/coraltemp_v3.1_20240923.nc"
clim_file    <- "data/coraltemp/ct5km_climatology_v3.1.nc"

s0 <- read_sst_window(one_sst_file, x_start_sst, y_start_sst, x_n_sst, y_n_sst)

a0 <- read_mmm_aligned_to_sst(
  clim_file = clim_file,
  lon_s = s0$lon_s,
  lat_s = s0$lat_s,
  sst_mat = s0$sst
)

lon_grid <- a0$lon_s   # length 90, ordered to match x=1..90
lat_grid <- a0$lat_s   # length 82, ordered to match y=1..82

# Lookup table mapping your (x,y) indices to lon/lat
pix_lut <- tidyr::crossing(
  x = seq_along(lon_grid),
  y = seq_along(lat_grid)
) %>%
  mutate(
    lon = lon_grid[x],
    lat = lat_grid[y]
  )

# Attach lon/lat to your dhw output
dhw_ll <- dhw_long_all %>%
  left_join(pix_lut, by = c("x","y"))

stopifnot(!anyNA(dhw_ll$lon), !anyNA(dhw_ll$lat))

tail(dhw_ll)

dhw_ll %>%
  filter(date == "2023-09-15")


peak_dhw23 <- dhw_ll %>%
  filter(year == 2023) %>%
  group_by(lon, lat) %>%
  summarize(peak = max(dhw, na.rm = TRUE))
ggplot(peak_dhw23, aes(x = lon, y = lat, fill = peak)) +
  geom_tile()


peak_dhw23_round <- peak_dhw23 %>%
  mutate(
    lon = round(lon, 2),
    lat = round(lat, 2)
  )


# pick the same date you plotted from CRW, or compute peak date first

# create a raster from points (lon/lat are pixel centers)
dhw_rast <- terra::rast(peak_dhw23_round, type = "xyz", crs = "EPSG:4326")
names(dhw_rast) <- "dhw"

# optional: snap to CRW raster grid if you want exact alignment
# (recommended if you will compare cell-by-cell)
dhw_rast <- terra::resample(dhw_rast, peak_dhw_reef, method = "near")

plot(dhw_rast)

```



# calc 2023 dhw from sst -- raster approach
```{r}
# 1) Read daily SST as a SpatRaster stack (cropped)
# You can still keep your folder-per-year organization, but you don’t have to.

sst_dir <- "data/coraltemp"
sst_files <- list.files(sst_dir, pattern="^coraltemp_v3\\.1_\\d{8}\\.nc$", full.names=TRUE)

sst_dates <- file_date(sst_files)
keep <- sst_dates >= as.Date("2023-06-01") - 83 & sst_dates <= as.Date("2023-12-31")

sst_files <- sst_files[keep]
sst_dates <- sst_dates[keep]

# analysed_sst is the variable name in coraltemp files
sst_stack <- rast(sst_files, subds="analysed_sst")
time(sst_stack) <- sst_dates

# crop once
sst_stack <- crop(sst_stack, fl_ext)

#2) Read MMM climatology as a SpatRaster, crop, align to SST grid
clim_file <- "data/coraltemp/ct5km_climatology_v3.1.nc"

mmm <- rast(clim_file, subds="sst_clim_mmm")
mmm <- crop(mmm, fl_ext)

# Force MMM onto the exact SST grid if needed:
# (this handles any tiny grid/origin differences cleanly)
mmm <- resample(mmm, sst_stack[[1]], method="bilinear")

# 3) Compute DHW rasters (year-specific MMM shift supported)
# Here’s a function that returns:
# dhw_stack (daily DHW within the analysis window)
# peak_dhw (max DHW)
# …and it takes mmm_add so you can test shifts.

compute_dhw_rasters <- function(sst_stack, mmm, 
                                start_date, end_date,
                                mmm_add = 0) {

  # add year-specific offset to MMM (your “tolerance shift”)
  mmm_shift <- mmm + mmm_add

  # HotSpot
  hotspot <- sst_stack - mmm_shift
  hotspot <- clamp(hotspot, lower=0, values=TRUE)

  # DHD contribution: if HotSpot >= 1 then HotSpot/7 else 0
  dhd <- hotspot / 7
  dhd <- ifel(hotspot >= 1, dhd, 0)

  # 84-day rolling sum (current day + previous 83)
  # terra::focal() works on one layer at a time; for time-rolling,
  # do it by indexing layers.
  n <- nlyr(dhd)
  w <- 84L

  # preallocate output stack (same geometry)
  dhw <- dhd[[1:w]]  # placeholder with w layers; we'll replace
  dhw <- dhw[[1]]; dhw <- dhw * NA  # single NA layer as template

  dhw_list <- vector("list", n)
  # Version requiring complete 84day windows
  # for (i in seq_len(n)) {
  #   if (i < w) {
  #     dhw_list[[i]] <- dhw  # NA until window complete
  #   } else {
  #     dhw_list[[i]] <- app(dhd[[(i-w+1):i]], sum, na.rm=TRUE)
  #   }
  # }
  # Version allowing incomplete windows
  for (i in seq_len(n)) {
    j0 <- max(1L, i - w + 1L)                 # start of available window
    dhw_list[[i]] <- app(dhd[[j0:i]], sum, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  dhw_stack <- rast(dhw_list)
  time(dhw_stack) <- time(dhd)

  # keep only analysis window (drop cushion)
  keep2 <- time(dhw_stack) >= start_date & time(dhw_stack) <= end_date
  dhw_stack <- dhw_stack[[which(keep2)]]

  peak_dhw <- app(dhw_stack, max, na.rm=TRUE)
  names(peak_dhw) <- "peak_dhw"

  list(dhw_stack = dhw_stack, peak_dhw = peak_dhw)
}

res23 <- compute_dhw_rasters(
  sst_stack  = sst_stack,
  mmm        = mmm,
  start_date = as.Date("2023-06-01"),
  end_date   = as.Date("2023-12-31"),
  mmm_add    = 0   # or try 0.2, 0.5, etc
)

peak_dhw_calc23 <- res23$peak_dhw
dhw_stack_calc <- res23$dhw_stack

# 4) Mask to reef hull and plot exactly like your CRW workflow
# First fix your CRS mismatch warning (same as with CRW):
# make reef hull a SpatVector in the raster CRS
reef_v <- vect(reef_hull)
reef_v <- project(reef_v, crs(peak_dhw_calc23))

peak_dhw_calc23_reef <- mask(peak_dhw_calc23, reef_v)

library(tidyterra)
library(ggplot2)

dhwmap_calc23 <- basemap +
  geom_spatraster(data = peak_dhw_calc23_reef) +
  geom_sf(data = world, linewidth = 0.1, fill = "gray90") +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-83.2, -79.8), ylim = c(24.3, 27.3), expand = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "Spectral",
    limits = c(0, 23),
    na.value = NA,
    name = "Peak\nDHW "
  )

dhwmap_calc23
```

```{r}
# Get DHW values for 2023 DRM surveys

drmsites <- read_csv("data/drm/export.csv") %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), as_factor),
         Date = as_date(as.character(Date), format = "%m/%d/%Y")) %>%
  filter(year(Date) == 2023) %>%
  distinct(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude)

library(terra)

buf_m <- 6000

extract_dhw_terra <- function(r, df_xy, buf = buf_m) {
  v <- terra::vect(df_xy, geom = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = "EPSG:4326")
  v <- terra::project(v, crs(r))  # make sure CRS matches raster

  # nearest cell
  v_simple <- terra::extract(r, v, method = "simple")[,2]

  # buffered mean
  v_buf <- terra::extract(r, v, buffer = buf, fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)[,2]

  ifelse(is.na(v_simple), v_buf, v_simple)
}

# your computed DHW daily stack for the analysis window
dhw_stack_calc23 <- res23$dhw_stack

dates <- time(dhw_stack_calc23)

out2_calc <- tibble(
  date = dates,
  lyr  = seq_along(dates)
) %>%
  mutate(
    dhw = purrr::map(lyr, ~extract_dhw_terra(dhw_stack_calc23[[.x]], drmsites, buf = buf_m)),
    site = list(drmsites %>% dplyr::select(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude)),
    n_dhw = purrr::map_int(dhw, length),
    n_site = purrr::map_int(site, nrow)
  ) %>%
  filter(n_dhw == n_site) %>%
  dplyr::select(date, site, dhw) %>%
  tidyr::unnest(cols = c(site, dhw))


# Plot DHWs for all sites
ggplot(out2_calc, aes(x = date, y = dhw, color = Subregion, group = Site)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "month", date_labels = "%b")


write_csv(out2_calc, file = "data/processed/2023_dhw_calc_processed.csv")
```




# 2015

```{r}
# Import all NetCDF files for each date from June 1 through December 31, 2015
nc.files <- list.files("data/dhw/2015", pattern = "*.nc$", full.names = TRUE)

# Get coordinate box for Florida Reef Tract
#nc <- nc_open("data/dhw/ct5km_dhw_v3.1_20230901.nc")
#nct <- ncvar_get(nc, "degree_heating_week", start = c(2000, 1240,1), count = c(21, 81,1))
# Longitude -83.525 --> index 1930
#nc$dim$lon$vals[2021]
# Longitude -78.975 --> index 2021
# Latitude 24 --> index 1321
#nc$dim$lat$vals[1240]
# Latitude 28 --> index 1240

# Get DRM survey site coordinates
# Import data
drmsites <- read_csv("data/drm/export.csv") %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), as_factor),
         Date = as_date(as.character(Date), format = "%m/%d/%Y")) %>%
  filter(year(Date) == "2015") %>%
  distinct(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude)

# Open all DHW NCDF4 files
nc.data <- tibble(file = nc.files) %>%
  mutate(date = map_chr(file, ~str_extract(., "2015\\d+"), format = "%Y%m%d"),
         data = map(file, nc_open),
      dhw.all = map(data, ~ncvar_get(., "degree_heating_week", start = c(1930, 1240, 1), count = c(91, 81, 1))),
            r = map(dhw.all, ~raster(t(.), xmn = -83.525, xmx = -78.975, ymn = 23.975, ymx = 28.025,
                                     crs = CRS("+proj=longlat +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +no_defs+ towgs84=0,0,0"))),
       coords = list(drmsites),
          dhw = map2(r, coords, ~raster::extract(.x, SpatialPoints(.y[,3:4]), method = "simple")))

###### GET NEAREST PIXEL INSTEAD OF NEAREST SITE
buf_m <- 6000  # 

extract_dhw <- function(r, df_xy, buf = buf_m) {
  sp <- SpatialPoints(df_xy[, c("Longitude","Latitude")], proj4string = crs(r))

  v_simple <- raster::extract(r, sp, method = "simple")

  # buffered mean ignoring NA; will still be NA if *all* nearby cells are NA
  v_buf <- raster::extract(r, sp, buffer = buf, fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)

  # use simple unless it's NA, then buffered
  ifelse(is.na(v_simple), v_buf, v_simple)
}

nc.data <- nc.data %>%
  mutate(
    dhw = map(r, ~extract_dhw(.x, drmsites, buf = buf_m))
  )

out2 <- nc.data %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(date, "%Y%m%d"),
    site = list(drmsites %>% dplyr::select(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude)),
    # sanity check: dhw vector length should equal nrow(drmsites)
    n_dhw = map_int(dhw, length),
    n_site = map_int(site, nrow)
  ) %>%
  # optional: stop early if something doesn't match
  filter(n_dhw == n_site) %>%
  dplyr::select(date, site, dhw) %>%
  unnest(cols = c(site, dhw))
```

```{r}
# Plot DHWs for all sites
ggplot(out2, aes(x = date, y = dhw, color = Subregion, group = Site)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "month", date_labels = "%b")
```

```{r}
# Write DHW data to file
write_csv(out2, file = "data/processed/2015_dhw_processed.csv")
```

```{r}
# Map DHW for all reef pixels not just surveyed sites
# Read DHW from all files as a multi-layer SpatRaster
# (each file contributes one layer; varname must match exactly)
dhw_stack <- rast(nc.files, subds = "degree_heating_week")

# Optional: keep only June 1–Dec 31 if your folder includes other dates
# (assumes filename contains YYYYMMDD like ct5km_dhw_v3.1_20230901.nc)
dates <- as.Date(str_extract(basename(nc.files), "2015\\d{4}"), format = "%Y%m%d")
keep  <- dates >= as.Date("2015-06-01") & dates <= as.Date("2015-12-31")
dhw_stack <- dhw_stack[[which(keep)]]

# Crop to your Florida Reef Tract box (match your plotting extent-ish)
dhw_stack <- crop(dhw_stack, ext(-83.525, -78.975, 23.975, 28.025))
dhw_stack_crw_2015 <- dhw_stack

# Peak DHW (max over time) for each pixel
peak_dhw <- app(dhw_stack, fun = max, na.rm = TRUE)
names(peak_dhw) <- "peak_dhw"


# Mask to reef pixels hull
peak_dhw_reef <- mask(peak_dhw, vect(reef_hull))

# Plot as heatmap
dhwmap_pixels15 <- basemap +
  geom_spatraster(data = peak_dhw_reef) +   # or peak_dhw if you don't mask
  geom_sf(data = world, linewidth = 0.1, fill = "gray90") +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-83.2, -79.8), ylim = c(24.3, 27.3), expand = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "Spectral",
    limits = c(0, 23),
    na.value = NA,
    name = "Peak\nDHW      "
  )

dhwmap_pixels15
```

# 2014
```{r}
# Import all NetCDF files for each date from June 1 through December 31, 2015
nc.files <- list.files("data/dhw/2014", pattern = "*.nc$", full.names = TRUE)

# Get coordinate box for Florida Reef Tract
#nc <- nc_open("data/dhw/ct5km_dhw_v3.1_20230901.nc")
#nct <- ncvar_get(nc, "degree_heating_week", start = c(2000, 1240,1), count = c(21, 81,1))
# Longitude -83.525 --> index 1930
#nc$dim$lon$vals[2021]
# Longitude -78.975 --> index 2021
# Latitude 24 --> index 1321
#nc$dim$lat$vals[1240]
# Latitude 28 --> index 1240

# Get DRM survey site coordinates
# Import data
drmsites <- read_csv("data/drm/export.csv") %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), as_factor),
         Date = as_date(as.character(Date), format = "%m/%d/%Y")) %>%
  filter(year(Date) == "2014") %>%
  distinct(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude)

# Open all DHW NCDF4 files
nc.data <- tibble(file = nc.files) %>%
  mutate(date = map_chr(file, ~str_extract(., "2014\\d+"), format = "%Y%m%d"),
         data = map(file, nc_open),
      dhw.all = map(data, ~ncvar_get(., "degree_heating_week", start = c(1930, 1240, 1), count = c(91, 81, 1))),
            r = map(dhw.all, ~raster(t(.), xmn = -83.525, xmx = -78.975, ymn = 23.975, ymx = 28.025,
                                     crs = CRS("+proj=longlat +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +no_defs+ towgs84=0,0,0"))),
       coords = list(drmsites),
          dhw = map2(r, coords, ~raster::extract(.x, SpatialPoints(.y[,3:4]), method = "simple")))


###### GET NEAREST PIXEL INSTEAD OF NEAREST SITE
buf_m <- 6000  # 

extract_dhw <- function(r, df_xy, buf = buf_m) {
  sp <- SpatialPoints(df_xy[, c("Longitude","Latitude")], proj4string = crs(r))

  v_simple <- raster::extract(r, sp, method = "simple")

  # buffered mean ignoring NA; will still be NA if *all* nearby cells are NA
  v_buf <- raster::extract(r, sp, buffer = buf, fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)

  # use simple unless it's NA, then buffered
  ifelse(is.na(v_simple), v_buf, v_simple)
}

nc.data <- nc.data %>%
  mutate(
    dhw = map(r, ~extract_dhw(.x, drmsites, buf = buf_m))
  )

out2 <- nc.data %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(date, "%Y%m%d"),
    site = list(drmsites %>% dplyr::select(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude)),
    # sanity check: dhw vector length should equal nrow(drmsites)
    n_dhw = map_int(dhw, length),
    n_site = map_int(site, nrow)
  ) %>%
  # optional: stop early if something doesn't match
  filter(n_dhw == n_site) %>%
  dplyr::select(date, site, dhw) %>%
  unnest(cols = c(site, dhw))
```

```{r}
# Plot DHWs for all sites
ggplot(out2, aes(x = date, y = dhw, color = Subregion, group = Site)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "month", date_labels = "%b")
```

```{r}
# Write DHW data to file
write_csv(out2, file = "data/processed/2014_dhw_processed.csv")
```

```{r}
# Map DHW for all reef pixels not just surveyed sites
# Read DHW from all files as a multi-layer SpatRaster
# (each file contributes one layer; varname must match exactly)
dhw_stack <- rast(nc.files, subds = "degree_heating_week")

# Optional: keep only June 1–Dec 31 if your folder includes other dates
# (assumes filename contains YYYYMMDD like ct5km_dhw_v3.1_20230901.nc)
dates <- as.Date(str_extract(basename(nc.files), "2014\\d{4}"), format = "%Y%m%d")
keep  <- dates >= as.Date("2014-06-01") & dates <= as.Date("2014-12-31")
dhw_stack <- dhw_stack[[which(keep)]]

# Crop to your Florida Reef Tract box (match your plotting extent-ish)
dhw_stack <- crop(dhw_stack, ext(-83.525, -78.975, 23.975, 28.025))
dhw_stack_crw_2014 <- dhw_stack

# Peak DHW (max over time) for each pixel
peak_dhw <- app(dhw_stack, fun = max, na.rm = TRUE)
names(peak_dhw) <- "peak_dhw"


# Mask to reef pixels hull
peak_dhw_reef <- mask(peak_dhw, vect(reef_hull))

# Plot as heatmap
dhwmap_pixels14 <- basemap +
  geom_spatraster(data = peak_dhw_reef) +   # or peak_dhw if you don't mask
  geom_sf(data = world, linewidth = 0.1, fill = "gray90") +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-83.2, -79.8), ylim = c(24.3, 27.3), expand = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "Spectral",
    limits = c(0, 23),
    na.value = NA,
    name = "Peak\nDHW      "
  )

dhwmap_pixels14
```

# 2024

```{r}
# Import all NetCDF files for each date from June 1 through December 31, 2024
nc.files <- list.files("data/dhw/2024", pattern = "*.nc$", full.names = TRUE)

# Get DRM survey site coordinates
# Import data
drmsites <- read_csv("data/drm/FRRP_EXPORT.csv") %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), as_factor),
         Date = as_date(as.character(Date), format = "%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S")) %>%
  filter(year(Date) == "2024") %>%
  distinct(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude)

# Open all DHW NCDF4 files
nc.data <- tibble(file = nc.files) %>%
  mutate(date = map_chr(file, ~str_extract(., "2024\\d+"), format = "%Y%m%d"),
         data = map(file, nc_open),
      dhw.all = map(data, ~ncvar_get(., "degree_heating_week", start = c(1930, 1240, 1), count = c(91, 81, 1))),
            r = map(dhw.all, ~raster(t(.), xmn = -83.525, xmx = -78.975, ymn = 23.975, ymx = 28.025,
                                     crs = CRS("+proj=longlat +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +no_defs+ towgs84=0,0,0"))),
       coords = list(drmsites),
          dhw = map2(r, coords, ~raster::extract(.x, SpatialPoints(.y[,3:4]), method = "simple")))

# Get dhw data for all site coords
# use a buffer of 5km to get nearest data for sites that are outside data pixels
buf_m <- 5000  # 

extract_dhw <- function(r, df_xy, buf = buf_m) {
  sp <- SpatialPoints(df_xy[, c("Longitude","Latitude")], proj4string = crs(r))

  v_simple <- raster::extract(r, sp, method = "simple")

  # buffered mean ignoring NA; will still be NA if *all* nearby cells are NA
  v_buf <- raster::extract(r, sp, buffer = buf, fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)

  # use simple unless it's NA, then buffered
  ifelse(is.na(v_simple), v_buf, v_simple)
}

nc.data <- nc.data %>%
  mutate(
    dhw = map(r, ~extract_dhw(.x, drmsites, buf = buf_m))
  )

out2 <- nc.data %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(date, "%Y%m%d"),
    site = list(drmsites %>% dplyr::select(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude)),
    # sanity check: dhw vector length should equal nrow(drmsites)
    n_dhw = map_int(dhw, length),
    n_site = map_int(site, nrow)
  ) %>%
  # optional: stop early if something doesn't match
  filter(n_dhw == n_site) %>%
  dplyr::select(date, site, dhw) %>%
  unnest(cols = c(site, dhw))

# re-check missing sites
dhws <- out2 %>% group_by(Site, Latitude, Longitude) %>% summarize(maxdhw = max(dhw, na.rm = TRUE), .groups="drop")
nodhw <- dhws %>% filter(is.na(maxdhw))
nodhw


```

```{r}
# Plot DHWs for all sites
ggplot(out2, aes(x = date, y = dhw, color = Subregion, group = Site)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "month", date_labels = "%b")
```

```{r}
# Write DHW data to file
write_csv(out2, file = "data/processed/2024_dhw_processed.csv")
```

```{r}
# Map DHW for all reef pixels not just surveyed sites
# Read DHW from all files as a multi-layer SpatRaster
# (each file contributes one layer; varname must match exactly)
dhw_stack <- rast(nc.files, subds = "degree_heating_week")

# Optional: keep only June 1–Dec 31 if your folder includes other dates
# (assumes filename contains YYYYMMDD like ct5km_dhw_v3.1_20230901.nc)
dates <- as.Date(str_extract(basename(nc.files), "2024\\d{4}"), format = "%Y%m%d")
keep  <- dates >= as.Date("2024-06-01") & dates <= as.Date("2024-12-31")
dhw_stack <- dhw_stack[[which(keep)]]

# Crop to your Florida Reef Tract box (match your plotting extent-ish)
dhw_stack <- crop(dhw_stack, ext(-83.525, -78.975, 23.975, 28.025))

# Peak DHW (max over time) for each pixel
peak_dhw <- app(dhw_stack, fun = max, na.rm = TRUE)
names(peak_dhw) <- "peak_dhw"


# Mask to reef pixels hull
peak_dhw_reef <- mask(peak_dhw, vect(reef_hull))

# Plot as heatmap
dhwmap_pixels24 <- basemap +
  geom_spatraster(data = peak_dhw_reef) +   # or peak_dhw if you don't mask
  geom_sf(data = world, linewidth = 0.1, fill = "gray90") +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-83.2, -79.8), ylim = c(24.3, 27.3), expand = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "Spectral",
    limits = c(0, 23),
    na.value = NA,
    name = "Peak\nDHW      "
  )

dhwmap_pixels24
```


```{r}
cowplot::plot_grid(dhwmap_pixels14, dhwmap_pixels15,
                   dhwmap_pixels23, dhwmap_pixels24,
                   ncol = 2,
                   labels = c("2014", "2015", "2023", "2024"),
                   label_x = 0.1, label_y = 0.92)
ggsave(filename = "figures/FigS2.png", width = 200, height = 185, units = "mm")
```

# FULL NEW WORKFLOW
```{r}
# ============================================================
# Compute DHW from CoralTemp SST + MMM for multiple years,
# (2014, 2015, 2023, 2024), make peak-DHW maps, and export
# site-extracted daily DHW ("out2-style") CSVs per year.
# ============================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(stringr)
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(tidyterra)
library(rnaturalearth)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 0) Base map + reef hull (as you already had)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
world <- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(scale = "large", returnclass = "sf")

basemap <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world, lwd = 0.1, fill = "gray90") +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-83.2, -79.8), ylim = c(24.3, 27.3), expand = FALSE) +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.title = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "lightsteelblue1"),
        panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill=NA),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = c(0.2, 0.5),
        legend.background = element_blank())

# Reef hull/polygon surrounding all surveyed sites (saved for reuse)
all_survey_pts0 <- read_csv("data/drm/FRRP_EXPORT.csv") %>%
  distinct(Latitude, Longitude)

all_survey_pts <- sf::st_as_sf(all_survey_pts0, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326)
reef_hull <- sf::st_concave_hull(sf::st_union(all_survey_pts), ratio = 0.09) |>
  sf::st_buffer(dist = 0.015) |>
  sf::st_set_crs(4326)

saveRDS(reef_hull, "data/spatial/reef_hull.rds")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Helpers + constants
# ------------------------------------------------------------
file_date <- function(f) as.Date(str_match(basename(f), "(\\d{8})")[,2], "%Y%m%d")

years_keep <- c(2014, 2015, 2023, 2024)
start_mmdd <- "06-01"
end_mmdd   <- "12-31"
cushion_days <- 83L          # needed for full 84d windows; we still load this for stability
buf_m <- 6000                # used only if NA at nearest cell
buf_deg <- 0.05
fl_ext <- ext(-83.525, -78.975, 23.975, 28.025)

sst_dir   <- "data/coraltemp"
clim_file <- "data/coraltemp/ct5km_climatology_v3.1.nc"

# year-specific MMM offsets (edit these as you test tolerance shifts)
add_by_year <- c("2014"=0, "2015"=0, "2023"=0, "2024"=0)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Load MMM first (as the "canonical" grid), crop once
#    Then we will resample SST stacks onto MMM grid each year.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
mmm0 <- rast(clim_file, subds = "sst_clim_mmm") |>
  crop(fl_ext)
terra::crs(mmm0) <- "EPSG:4326"

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) DHW computation (raster-native; partial windows allowed)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
compute_dhw_rasters <- function(sst_stack_on_mmm, mmm_on_grid,
                                start_date, end_date,
                                mmm_add = 0) {

  mmm_shift <- mmm_on_grid + mmm_add

  # HotSpot
  hotspot <- sst_stack_on_mmm - mmm_shift
  hotspot <- clamp(hotspot, lower = 0, values = TRUE)

  # DHD contribution
  dhd <- hotspot / 7
  dhd <- ifel(hotspot >= 1, dhd, 0)

  # 84-day rolling sum (partial windows allowed)
  n <- nlyr(dhd)
  w <- 84L
  dhw_list <- vector("list", n)

  for (i in seq_len(n)) {
    j0 <- max(1L, i - w + 1L)
    dhw_list[[i]] <- app(dhd[[j0:i]], sum, na.rm = TRUE)
  }

  dhw_stack <- rast(dhw_list)
  time(dhw_stack) <- time(dhd)

  keep <- time(dhw_stack) >= start_date & time(dhw_stack) <= end_date
  dhw_stack <- dhw_stack[[which(keep)]]

  peak_dhw <- app(dhw_stack, max, na.rm = TRUE)
  names(peak_dhw) <- "peak_dhw"

  list(dhw_stack = dhw_stack, peak_dhw = peak_dhw)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Site extraction helper (nearest cell, fallback to buffered mean)
# NOTE: buffer units follow raster CRS. If rasters are EPSG:4326,
#       buffer is in degrees. This keeps your prior behavior, but
#       if you truly want meters, project both raster+points first.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
extract_dhw_terra <- function(r, df_xy, buf = buf_m) {
  v <- terra::vect(df_xy, geom = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = "EPSG:4326")
  r_crs <- terra::crs(r)

  # If raster CRS is that GEOGCRS["unknown", ...] thing, treat it as EPSG:4326
  if (is.na(r_crs) || r_crs == "" || grepl('GEOGCRS\\["unknown"', r_crs)) {
   terra::crs(r) <- "EPSG:4326"
  }

  v <- terra::vect(df_xy, geom = c("Longitude","Latitude"), crs = "EPSG:4326")
  # now CRS matches; no need to project v at all

  v_simple <- terra::extract(r, v, method = "simple")[,2]
  v_buf    <- terra::extract(r, v, buffer = buf, fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)[,2]
  v_buf <- terra::extract(r, terra::buffer(v, width = 6000), fun = base::mean, na.rm = TRUE)[,2]

  #ifelse(is.na(v_simple), v_buf, v_simple)
  v_simple
}

buf_m <- 6000

extract_dhw_rastermatch <- function(r, df_xy, buf = buf_m) {
  sp <- sp::SpatialPoints(df_xy[, c("Longitude", "Latitude")], proj4string = raster::crs(r))

  v_simple <- raster::extract(r, sp, method = "simple")

  # buffered mean ignoring NA; still NA if all nearby cells are NA
  v_buf <- raster::extract(r, sp, buffer = buf, fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)

  ifelse(is.na(v_simple), v_buf, v_simple)
}

buf_m <- 6000

extract_dhw_rastermatch_stack <- function(rstk, df_xy, buf = buf_m) {
  sp <- sp::SpatialPoints(df_xy[, c("Longitude", "Latitude")], proj4string = raster::crs(rstk))

  # simple extraction for all layers at once
  v_simple <- raster::extract(rstk, sp, method = "simple")

  # buffered mean for all layers at once
  v_buf <- raster::extract(rstk, sp, buffer = buf, fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)

  # use simple unless NA, then buffered
  out <- v_simple
  #out[is.na(out)] <- v_buf[is.na(out)]

  out
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) All available SST files once
# ------------------------------------------------------------
sst_files_all <- list.files(sst_dir, pattern="^coraltemp_v3\\.1_\\d{8}\\.nc$", full.names=TRUE)
sst_dates_all <- file_date(sst_files_all)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) Loop years: build SST stack -> resample to MMM grid -> compute DHW
#    -> peak map (reef-masked) -> extract to sites -> write CSV
# ------------------------------------------------------------
dir.create("data/processed", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

dhw_stacks_calc <- list()
plots <- list()

for (yy in years_keep) {

  message("Year: ", yy)

  start_date <- as.Date(sprintf("%d-%s", yy, start_mmdd))
  end_date   <- as.Date(sprintf("%d-%s", yy, end_mmdd))

  # SST files for this year, including cushion days before start_date
  keep <- sst_dates_all >= (start_date - cushion_days) & sst_dates_all <= end_date
  sst_files <- sst_files_all[keep]
  sst_dates <- sst_dates_all[keep]

  if (length(sst_files) == 0) stop("No SST files found for year ", yy)

  # Read SST stack, crop, then resample onto MMM grid (canonical)
  sst_stack <- rast(sst_files, subds = "analysed_sst")
  time(sst_stack) <- sst_dates
  sst_stack <- crop(sst_stack, fl_ext)

  # Put SST on MMM grid (so comparisons across years are identical geometry)
  sst_on_mmm <- resample(sst_stack, mmm0, method = "bilinear")
  terra::crs(sst_on_mmm) <- "EPSG:4326"

  # DHW compute (year-specific MMM shift)
  add_val <- add_by_year[[as.character(yy)]] %||% 0
  res <- compute_dhw_rasters(
    sst_stack_on_mmm = sst_on_mmm,
    mmm_on_grid      = mmm0,
    start_date       = start_date,
    end_date         = end_date,
    mmm_add          = add_val
  )

  # Reef-mask peak DHW and build map
  reef_v <- vect(reef_hull) |> project(crs(res$peak_dhw))
  peak_reef <- mask(res$peak_dhw, reef_v)

  p <- basemap +
    geom_spatraster(data = peak_reef) +
    geom_sf(data = world, linewidth = 0.1, fill = "gray90") +
    coord_sf(xlim = c(-83.2, -79.8), ylim = c(24.3, 27.3), expand = FALSE) +
    scale_fill_distiller(
      palette = "Spectral",
      limits = c(0, 23),
      na.value = NA,
      name = "Peak\nDHW "
    ) +
    ggtitle(paste0("Peak DHW (computed) ", yy))

  plots[[as.character(yy)]] <- p

  # DRM sites for that year (out2-style daily extraction)
  drmsites <- read_csv("data/drm/FRRP_EXPORT.csv") %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.character), as_factor),
           Date = as_date(as.character(Date), format = "%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S")) %>%
    filter(year(Date) == yy) %>%
    distinct(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude)

  dhw_stack <- res$dhw_stack
  
  # Save stack for later comparison
  dhw_stacks_calc[[as.character(yy)]] <- dhw_stack

  # Also assign to global env for convenience
  assign(paste0("dhw_stack_calc_", yy), dhw_stack, envir = .GlobalEnv)
  
  dates <- time(dhw_stack)

  # out2_calc <- tibble(date = dates, lyr = seq_along(dates)) %>%
  #   mutate(
  #     #dhw  = map(lyr, ~extract_dhw_terra(dhw_stack[[.x]], drmsites, buf = buf_m)),
  #     dhw = map(lyr, ~extract_dhw_rastermatch(raster::raster(dhw_stack[[.x]]), drmsites, buf = buf_m)),
  #     site = list(drmsites %>% dplyr::select(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude)),
  #     n_dhw  = map_int(dhw, length),
  #     n_site = map_int(site, nrow)
  #   ) %>%
  #   filter(n_dhw == n_site) %>%
  #   dplyr::select(date, site, dhw) %>%
  #   unnest(cols = c(site, dhw))
  
  dhw_stack_raster <- raster::stack(dhw_stack)
  mat_dhw <- extract_dhw_rastermatch_stack(dhw_stack_raster, drmsites, buf = buf_m)
  colnames(mat_dhw) <- seq_len(ncol(mat_dhw))

  out2_calc <- as_tibble(mat_dhw) %>%
    mutate(
      Site = drmsites$Site,
      Subregion = drmsites$Subregion,
      Longitude = drmsites$Longitude,
      Latitude = drmsites$Latitude
    ) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -c(Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude),
      names_to = "lyr",
      values_to = "dhw"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      lyr = as.integer(lyr),
      date = dates[lyr]
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(date, Site, Subregion, Longitude, Latitude, dhw) %>%
    arrange(date, Site)

  write_csv(out2_calc, file = sprintf("data/processed/%d_dhw_calc_processed.csv", yy))
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 7) Print maps (in your Rmd, these will display)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
plots[["2014"]]
plots[["2015"]]
plots[["2023"]]
plots[["2024"]]
```

# compare extracted values
```{r}
dhw14.1 <- read_csv("data/processed/2014_dhw_processed.csv")
dhw14.2 <- read_csv("data/processed/2014_dhw_calc_processed.csv")

dhw14 <- full_join(dhw14.1, dhw14.2, by = c("date", "Site", "Subregion", "Longitude", "Latitude"))

mm <- dhw14 %>%
  filter(abs(dhw.x - dhw.y) > 0.5)

mm %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = dhw.x, y = dhw.y - dhw.x)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Site)) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

m2294 <- dhw14 %>% filter(Site == "M2294")
#m2294, june 25, 2014. CRW = 0.15, calc DHW = 0....
# what pixel is that in?




ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = filter(dhw14.1, Site == "M2294"), aes(x = date, y = dhw)) +
  geom_line(data = filter(dhw14.2, Site == "M2294"), aes(x = date, y = dhw), color = "red")
```

# compare whole stack
```{r}
dhw_stack_crw_2023
dhw_stack_calc_2023

# ============================================================
# 1. Ensure layers correspond to the same dates
# ============================================================

stopifnot(all(time(dhw_stack_crw_2023) == time(dhw_stack_calc_2023)))

# ============================================================
# 2. Pixel-wise difference stack
# ============================================================

dhw_diff_2023 <- dhw_stack_calc_2023 - dhw_stack_crw_2023
dhw_diff_2014 <- dhw_stack_calc_2014 - dhw_stack_crw_2014
dhw_diff_2015 <- dhw_stack_calc_2015 - dhw_stack_crw_2015

# summary statistics across the whole cube
global(dhw_diff_2023, c("min","mean","max"), na.rm = TRUE)
global(dhw_diff_2014, c("min","mean","max"), na.rm = TRUE)
global(dhw_diff_2015, c("min","mean","max"), na.rm = TRUE)


n <- nlyr(dhw_stack_calc_2023)

# no shift
summary(global(dhw_stack_calc_2023 - dhw_stack_crw_2023, c("min","mean","max"), na.rm = TRUE))

# calc vs CRW shifted forward 1 day
summary(global(dhw_stack_calc_2023[[1:(n-1)]] - dhw_stack_crw_2023[[2:n]],
       c("min","mean","max"), na.rm = TRUE))

# calc vs CRW shifted backward 1 day
summary(global(dhw_stack_calc_2023[[2:n]] - dhw_stack_crw_2023[[1:(n-1)]],
       c("min","mean","max"), na.rm = TRUE))

# ============================================================
# 3. Time series of mean difference across region
# ============================================================

diff_ts <- tibble(
  date = time(dhw_diff),
  mean_diff = global(dhw_diff, "mean", na.rm = TRUE)[,1],
  max_diff  = global(dhw_diff, "max", na.rm = TRUE)[,1],
  min_diff  = global(dhw_diff, "min", na.rm = TRUE)[,1]
)

ggplot(diff_ts, aes(date, mean_diff)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(y = "Mean DHW difference (calc − CRW)",
       title = "Regional mean difference in DHW")

# ============================================================
# 4. Spatial map of peak difference
# ============================================================

peak_diff <- app(dhw_diff, max, na.rm = TRUE)

plot(peak_diff,
     main = "Maximum difference (calc − CRW)")

# ggplot version if you prefer
basemap +
  geom_spatraster(data = peak_diff) +
  scale_fill_distiller(
    palette = "Spectral",
    limits = c(-5,5),
    name = "Peak ΔDHW"
  )

# ============================================================
# 5. Scatter comparison across ALL pixels and days
# ============================================================

df_compare <- as.data.frame(
  c(dhw_stack_crw, dhw_stack_calc),
  na.rm = TRUE
)

names(df_compare) <- c("crw","calc")

ggplot(df_compare, aes(crw, calc)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.05) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(title = "Pixel-by-pixel comparison")

# ============================================================
# 6. Where differences occur (fraction of grid cells)
# ============================================================

frac_diff <- global(abs(dhw_diff) > 0.001, "mean", na.rm = TRUE)

tibble(
  date = time(dhw_diff),
  frac_cells_different = frac_diff[,1]
) |>
  ggplot(aes(date, frac_cells_different)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(y = "Fraction of pixels with difference")
```
